mnemonics.title: PUE Remember
mnemonics.content: PUE = Power Used Effectively = Total Facility / IT Equipment. Lower is better!

mnemonics.title: Green Use Pillars
mnemonics.content: G-R-E-E-N Use: Green algorithms, Renewable energy, Energy Star, Efficient hardware, Normalize power settings, Use virtualization

acronyms.usage: Measures data center energy efficiency - lower is better (ideal is 1.0)
CPU power saving technique that adjusts voltage/frequency based on workload
Processor idle states where higher numbers mean more power saving
Software enabling multiple virtual machines on single physical hardware
Efficient use of IT resources to minimize environmental impact

visualTips.visualization: Draw a circle with Total Energy inside, IT Equipment at center. PUE = Outer circle / Inner circle. Lower outer circle = better efficiency!
Think of a staircase - C0 at top (fully awake), stepping down to C6 at bottom (deepest sleep). As you go down, energy saved increases but wake-up time increases too.
Imagine one big building (physical server) divided into many small apartments (VMs), each serving different families (applications) - saves land (servers) and resources!
Draw flow: Electricity → UPS → PDU → Servers → Return path. Cooling towers alongside. Label where energy is lost (inefficiencies).

analogies.analogy: PUE is like miles-per-gallon in a car. Just as higher MPG means better fuel efficiency, lower PUE means better energy efficiency for data centers.
Virtualization is like a bus that carries many passengers (virtual machines) instead of many empty cars (individual servers), saving fuel (energy) and road space (physical space).
DVFS is like reducing your car speed on a flat road to save fuel - you're still going to your destination but using less energy.
C-states are like different levels of sleep. Light sleep (C1) you can wake quickly, deep sleep (C6) saves more energy but takes longer to wake up.
